'''Greg Florimo''' (born 17 May 1967) is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer and current rugby league administrator known for his lifelong association with the [[North Sydney Bears]] both as a player and CEO.

==Early life==
Florimo began playing as a junior in the North Sydney district for [[City of Willoughby|Willoughby]] at age seven before playing for [[McMahons Point]], [[Crows Nest, New South Wales|Crows Nest]] and North Sydney Brothers. He also played for the [[Lane Cove Rugby Club]] Juniors.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://northern-district-times.whereilive.com.au/sport/story/players-launch-club-doco/ |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120825132250/http://northern-district-times.whereilive.com.au/sport/story/players-launch-club-doco/ |archive-date=25 August 2012 |title=Players launch club doco – Rugby Union – Sport – Northern District Times}}</ref> His secondary education was at North Sydney Boys High School.<ref>LinkedIn profile shows NSBHS 1979–84</ref>

==Playing career==
He played 285 first-grade games for the [[North Sydney Bears]] over 13 seasons, starting his career in the centres before moving to five-eighth. He made his début for the Bears in 1986 and retired in 1998. In first grade he scored 73 tries, 3 goals and 1 field goal. He famously called the North Sydney fans onto [[North Sydney Oval]] in 1997 after the game when Norths defeated traditional rivals [[Manly Sea Eagles|Manly]] 41–8.

Florimo's final game for Norths was in the 23–2 minor semi-final defeat by the [[Canterbury Bulldogs|Bulldogs]]. In total Florimo played 285 games for Norths which is more appearances than any other player at the club.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/seasons/nrl-1998/minor-semi/north-sydney-vs-canterbury/summary.html |title = NRL 1998 – Minor Semi – Rugby League Project}}</ref><ref>{{cite web| url = http://www.theroar.com.au/2017/06/10/north-sydney-bears-top-ten-players/| title = North Sydney Bears: Top ten Players}}</ref>

For [[New South Wales rugby league team|NSW]] he played four [[State of Origin series|State of Origin]] games between 1988 and 1995. He played four Tests for [[Australian Kangaroos|Australia]] between 1994 and 1995.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.eraofthebiff.com/p-int04.html |title=Greg Florimo Interview |access-date=11 May 2007 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071021035404/http://www.eraofthebiff.com/p-int04.html |archive-date=21 October 2007 |url-status=dead }}</ref> He played in the [[Super League]] for English clubs [[Wigan Warriors]] in 1999 and [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] in 2000.

Greg Florimo kicked two field goals in his senior rugby league career. The first came in a 19–10 win for North Sydney over [[Cronulla Sharks|Cronulla-Sutherland]] in Round 11 of the [[1991 NSWRL season|1991 season]]. The second came in the second test of the [[1995 Trans-Tasman Test series]] against [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] at the [[Sydney Football Stadium (1988)|Sydney Football Stadium]] in which Australia won 20–10 and wrapped up the series 2–0.

He was one of only two players ever to score a 10/10 performance in a match by ''[[Rugby League Week]]''. In August 2006 Florimo was named at centre in the North Sydney Bears' ''[[North Sydney Bears#Team of the Century|Team of the Century]]''. He is the brother-in-law of fellow former [[North Sydney Bears]] player [[Gary Larson (rugby league footballer)|Gary Larson]].

==Refereeing==
Florimo is also associated with the North Sydney Rugby League Referee's association, refereeing mini and mod footy on Saturday mornings.

==Post playing==
Florimo was at one stage the [[North Sydney Bears]] CEO, and is advocating a return for the club to the [[NRL]].<ref>{{cite web | url=http://north-shore-times.whereilive.com.au/sport/story/bears-in-talks-over-nrl-spot/ | title=North shore | News Local Newspapers North Shore Sydney | Local Community News NSW | Mosman Daily | North Shore Times | Daily Telegraph }}</ref> Florimo's son, Jay, was a member of the [[Wyong Roos]] squad in the [[NSW Cup]] competition (NSW Cup was formerly known as Reserve Grade).<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/newslocal/central-coast/wyong-roos-win-central-coast-rugby-league-grand-final/news-story/b67d43044b01e2339bd7ac545f9bf879 |accessdate=18 November 2021 |title=Wyong Roos win Central Coast Rugby League grand final |website=The Daily Telegraph | date=September 17, 2016 | author=Peter Way}}</ref>

On 25 January 2019, Florimo was announced as a Wellbeing, Education and Community Manager at the club.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.northsydneybears.com.au/new-role-for-club-legend/|title=Bears announce new role for club legend|website=North Sydney Bears|date=25 January 2019 }}</ref>
